title: Operation declaration
description: ONE named unit of mechanical work. The declaration is the SSOT both sides read: the control plane validates params and decides placement from it, the pod dispatches from it. Adding a tool = this file + one handler; nothing else may need to change, and the gates in tests/ enforce that.

op.description: Registry key, `domain.verb`. Also the join key into docs/CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.md (`executor.op`).

version.description: THIS op's version, independent of contracts/VERSION. contracts/VERSION pins the ENVELOPE/transport; an op evolving its params bumps only itself, and a NEW op is purely additive — no envelope bump. That split is what makes adding a tool cheap.

needs.description: Closed capability vocabulary. Placement is DERIVED from this plus `judgement` — never declared per-stage. A box that cannot supply a need cannot be handed the op.

judgement.description: True = this op DECIDES something (which keeps, which drops, pacing, tempo, shot planning, the b-roll judge, thresholds). A judgement op is control-plane-only BY CONSTRUCTION: the pod dispatcher refuses it and the placement gate refuses to route it, so the cut algorithm cannot reach a pod even if some future stage table says otherwise. This is the field that kills the render_base contradiction.

parity.description: How the local and pod transports are allowed to differ. An op that changes output MUST say so here — silence is not permitted.

inputs.description: Declared ports. A port is satisfied EITHER by a presigned URL (crossing a job boundary) or by a local path produced by an earlier op in the same chain on the same box — the second costs no R2 round-trip and is the normal case inside a job.

params.description: JSON Schema fragment for this op's params, validated by BOTH sides straight from here. There is nothing to mirror and no hand-written Pydantic model per op: the envelope stays closed, `params` is checked against this. MUST be a closed object — argv is code, and an open params bag is argv wearing a hat.

handler.description: `module:callable`, resolved inside the ops pack. EXACTLY ONE handler per op — LocalBackend imports it in-process and PodBackend sends typed params to the same symbol. Parity is structural, not tested into existence.

durable.description: Output only. True = must materialise to R2 (a deliverable, or something a LATER JOB reads). False = intra-job intermediate; it stays on the pod's local disk and the next op in the chain reads the path directly. THIS FLAG IS THE R2 BUDGET: docs/RENDER_FLEET_AND_4MIN_BUDGET.md measured ~10 min of a ~25 min pod run as per-stage R2 transport, so defaulting to false is what keeps op granularity from multiplying that.
